title: What Does It Cost?
author: Daniel Wen
body:
Jesus sat down and told a crowd of thousands: "Which of you, desiring to build a tower, does not first sit down and count the cost?" It is a strange thing to say in the middle of a call to follow Him. But Jesus was not discouraging them — He was being honest with them.

"For which of you, desiring to build a tower, does not first sit down and count the cost, whether he has enough to complete it?" — Luke 14:28

Shallow discipleship crumbles under pressure. Real following begins with eyes wide open. We give up comfort, control, and self-direction — and we gain something infinitely greater: a life that actually means something.

What has following Jesus cost you? What might He be asking you to lay down today?

title: Praying Without Borders
author: Daniel Wen
body:
Paul writes that his ambition was to preach the gospel "not where Christ has already been named, lest I build on someone else's foundation." He was driven to the frontier, to the places where no one had yet heard.

"Those who have never been told of him will see, and those who have never heard will understand." — Romans 15:21

Most of us will never move to an unreached people group. But every single believer has been given the same weapon Paul carried everywhere: prayer. When we pray for a missionary in Madagascar, we are not spectators — we are participants in the same mission.

Who are you praying for this week? If you are not yet on our Missions Matters prayer list, consider signing up and joining the whole church in lifting up our missionaries by name.

title: To the Ends of the Earth
author: Daniel Wen
body:
Before Jesus ascended, He gave His disciples a staggering promise: "You will be my witnesses in Jerusalem and in all Judea and Samaria, and to the end of the earth." Notice He did not say "try" or "maybe." He said you will.

"You will receive power when the Holy Spirit has come upon you, and you will be my witnesses..." — Acts 1:8

The mission of the church is not optional, and it is not ours alone to carry. The Spirit goes before us. The question is not whether God's purposes will advance — they will. The question is whether we will be part of it.

Today, take a moment to pray for one missionary by name. Ask God how He might use your ordinary life to serve His extraordinary mission.
